meaning: n.[军]放列动作,炮兵掩体,炮位,炮台
examples: Its emplacement age was Early Cretaceous.
This space of emplacement was opened up by Galileo.
But they differ in association and mode of emplacement.
Now mortally injured, he crawled towards the enemy emplacement and, when in position, threw a grenade into the bunker.
In another emplacement across the entrance to the bay, there is a cartoon of a jazz band - sadly, rather hidden by modern-day graffiti.
